message:
style(mux): cargo fmt import reordering + feat(termwiz): tile-based dirty region tracking

mux crate (14 files):
- cargo fmt reorders imports to standard Rust grouping convention across all mux
  source files (client, connui, domain, events, lib, localpane, pane, ssh,
  ssh_agent, tab, termwiztermtab, tmux, tmux_commands, tmux_pty). Pure style
  changes with no semantic impact.

termwiz widgets:
- Add DirtyRegionMode enum with Rects and Tiles variants, allowing callers to
  choose between per-cell dirty rectangles and tile-aligned dirty regions
- Add frame_dirty_tiles and frame_dirty_tile_cells to RenderTelemetry for
  reporting tile-level statistics alongside the existing rect-level counters
- Refactor render_to_screen_with_dirty_rects() to delegate to a new internal
  render_to_screen_with_dirty_region_mode() method parameterized by the mode
- Add public render_to_screen_with_dirty_tiles(tile_width, tile_height) that
  returns tile-aligned dirty regions, useful for GPU tile-upload optimizations
  where partial-screen updates need to be snapped to fixed-size tile boundaries
- Add test: render_to_screen_with_dirty_tiles_reports_frame_regions() verifying
  tile counting on first render and zero-tile on clean second render
- Update existing telemetry assertions to check new tile fields are zero when
  only rect mode is used

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
